combat_history.event: Both vessels reportedly used for supply runs to Russian forces, though specific operations remain classified.
combat_history.significance: First operational deployment, though limited compared to expectations for major amphibious operations

modernization.description: Replacement of Ukrainian engines with domestic alternatives due to sanctions
modernization.impact: Reduced reliability and performance compared to original design specifications

modernization.description: Integration of modern navigation and communication systems
modernization.impact: Modest improvement in situational awareness and interoperability

vulnerabilities.description: Minimal integrated air defense beyond short-range systems, vulnerable to modern anti-ship missiles
vulnerabilities.context: Cannot operate in contested airspace without significant escort protection
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Reliance on shore-based air cover and escort vessels

vulnerabilities.description: Poor integration between sensors, weapons, and command systems compared to Western equivalents
vulnerabilities.context: Limits operational effectiveness and situational awareness in complex environments

vulnerabilities.description: Limited class size and specialized systems create maintenance and logistics challenges
vulnerabilities.context: Sanctions have complicated spare parts and upgrade programs
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Increased focus on domestic alternatives, though with reduced capability

operational_patterns.typical_deployment: Regional presence missions and logistics support in Baltic and Black Sea regions
operational_patterns.typical_task_group: Operates independently or with frigate escort for longer deployments
operational_patterns.readiness_notes: Limited operational tempo due to small fleet size and maintenance requirements

peer_comparisons.key_differences: San Antonio-class features superior systems integration, stealth characteristics, and aviation facilities, but Ivan Gren-class has heavier defensive armament and simpler maintenance requirements
peer_comparisons.video_angle: East vs West amphibious warfare philosophy - sophistication versus ruggedness

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Chinese design is larger, more modern, and better integrated, representing more successful modernization of amphibious capabilities compared to Russian efforts
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Comparing Russian and Chinese approaches to naval modernization challenges

peer_comparisons.key_differences: The cancelled Mistral purchase would have provided superior aviation and command capabilities that Ivan Gren-class cannot match
peer_comparisons.video_angle: What Russia lost when the Mistral deal fell through - capability gap analysis

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Ivan Gren represents modest improvement in capacity and systems but not the generational leap originally envisioned
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Russian amphibious warfare evolution - incremental vs revolutionary change
